threats.title: Default Configuration Vulnerability – Root Access
threats.description: The system utilizes default configurations, potentially including default credentials or unnecessary privileges, allowing an attacker to gain elevated access and control.
threats.attack_vector: Exploitation of default credentials or vulnerabilities in the system's configuration to gain root or administrator access.
mitigations: Change all default credentials immediately.
Remove unnecessary services and accounts.
Implement strict access controls based on the principle of least privilege.

threats.title: Weak Authentication Mechanism
threats.description: The system lacks detailed authentication and authorization information, making it vulnerable to attackers impersonating legitimate users. Default credentials or weak password policies could be exploited.
threats.attack_vector: Brute-force attacks, credential stuffing, or direct exploitation of vulnerabilities in the authentication logic.  Lack of multi-factor authentication (MFA) exacerbates the risk.
mitigations: Implement strong password policies (complexity, length, rotation).
Enforce Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) for all users.
Regularly audit user accounts and permissions.

threats.title: Unvalidated Input – SQL Injection Vulnerability
threats.description: The system does not adequately sanitize or validate user input before using it in database queries. This could allow an attacker to inject malicious SQL code, potentially compromising the entire database.
threats.attack_vector: An attacker injects malicious SQL code into input fields (e.g., login forms, search boxes) that bypasses input validation and is executed by the database.
mitigations: Implement parameterized queries or prepared statements.
Use an Object-Relational Mapper (ORM) that handles input sanitization.
Employ a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to detect and block SQL injection attempts.

threats.title: Uncontrolled Resource Consumption – Brute Force Attack
threats.description: The system is vulnerable to brute-force attacks due to a lack of rate limiting and proper input validation, leading to excessive resource consumption and potential service disruption.
threats.attack_vector: An attacker repeatedly attempts to guess passwords or other credentials, overwhelming the system's resources and causing it to become unresponsive.
mitigations: Implement rate limiting on authentication attempts.
Use CAPTCHA or other challenge-response mechanisms.
Monitor system resources for suspicious activity.

threats.title: Exposed Sensitive Data in Logs
threats.description: System logs contain sensitive information, such as user credentials, internal IP addresses, or debugging details, which could be exposed if not properly secured.
threats.attack_vector: Unauthorized access to log files, either through direct access or exploitation of vulnerabilities in the logging mechanism.
mitigations: Implement strict access controls to log files.
Redact or mask sensitive data in logs.
Regularly review and audit log configurations.

mapped_threats.title: Default Configuration Vulnerability – Root Access
mapped_threats.description: The system utilizes default configurations, potentially including default credentials or unnecessary privileges, allowing an attacker to gain elevated access and control.
mapped_threats.attack_vector: Exploitation of default credentials or vulnerabilities in the system's configuration to gain root or administrator access.
mitigations: Change all default credentials immediately.
Remove unnecessary services and accounts.
Implement strict access controls based on the principle of least privilege.

mapped_threats.attack_vector: Brute-force attacks, credential stuffing, or direct exploitation of vulnerabilities in the authentication logic.  Lack of multi-factor authentication (MFA) exacerbates the risk.
mitigations: Implement strong password policies (complexity, length, rotation).

mapped_threats.title: Uncontrolled Resource Consumption – Brute Force Attack
mapped_threats.description: The system is vulnerable to brute-force attacks due to a lack of rate limiting and proper input validation, leading to excessive resource consumption and potential service disruption.
mapped_threats.attack_vector: An attacker repeatedly attempts to guess passwords or other credentials, overwhelming the system's resources and causing it to become unresponsive.
mitigations: Implement rate limiting on authentication attempts.
Use CAPTCHA or other challenge-response mechanisms.

mapped_threats.title: Exposed Sensitive Data in Logs
mapped_threats.description: System logs contain sensitive information, such as user credentials, internal IP addresses, or debugging details, which could be exposed if not properly secured.
mapped_threats.attack_vector: Unauthorized access to log files, either through direct access or exploitation of vulnerabilities in the logging mechanism.
mitigations: Implement strict access controls to log files.
Redact or mask sensitive data in logs.
Regularly review and audit log configurations.

gaps: Lack of comprehensive controls for resource consumption and denial of service.
Insufficient focus on data leakage prevention beyond log exposure.
priorities: High - Elevation of Privilege and Spoofing (Critical Severity) require immediate attention.
Medium - Denial of Service and Information Disclosure (High Severity) necessitate proactive mitigation strategies.

content.dfd_mermaid:
flowchart TB

    %% Styling
    classDef external fill:#e1f5fe,stroke:#0277bd,stroke-width:2px
    classDef process fill:#e8f5e9,stroke:#2e7d32,stroke-width:2px
    classDef datastore fill:#fff3e0,stroke:#ef6c00,stroke-width:2px
    classDef threat fill:#ffebee,stroke:#c62828,stroke-width:3px

    subgraph External["External Entities"]
        user[("👤 User")]
        admin[("👔 Admin")]
        external_api[("🌐 External API")]
    end

    subgraph TrustBoundary["Trust Boundary"]
        subgraph Frontend["Frontend Layer"]
            web_app["🖥️ Web Application"]
        end

        subgraph Backend["Backend Layer"]
            api_gateway["🚪 API Gateway"]
            auth_service["🔐 Auth Service"]
            core_api["⚙️ Core API"]
        end

        subgraph Data["Data Layer"]
            database[("💾 Database")]
            cache[("⚡ Cache")]
        end
    end

    %% Data Flows
    user -->|"🔒 HTTPS"| web_app
    admin -->|"🔒 HTTPS + MFA"| web_app
    web_app -->|"🔒 API Calls"| api_gateway
    api_gateway -->|"🔒 Auth Check"| auth_service
    api_gateway -->|"🔒 Business Logic"| core_api
    auth_service -->|"🔒 User Data"| database
    core_api -->|"🔒 CRUD"| database
    core_api -->|"⚠️ Session"| cache
    core_api -->|"🔒 External Call"| external_api

    %% Apply Styles
    class user,admin,external_api external
    class web_app,api_gateway,auth_service,core_api process
    class database,cache datastore
    class web_app,auth_service,core_api,database threat

semgrep.config_file:
rules:
- id: security-custom-1
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: "Default Configuration Vulnerability \u2013 Root Access"
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-269
    owasp: A01:2021
    threat_category: Elevation of Privilege
  patterns:
  - pattern: eval($X)
  - pattern: exec($X)
  severity: ERROR
- id: security-custom-2
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Weak Authentication Mechanism
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-287
    owasp: A07:2021
    threat_category: Spoofing
  patterns:
  - pattern: password = "..."
  - pattern: secret = "..."
  - pattern: api_key = "..."
  severity: ERROR
- fix: cursor.execute($QUERY, (params,))
  id: security-custom-3
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: "Unvalidated Input \u2013 SQL Injection Vulnerability"
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-89
    owasp: A03:2021
    threat_category: Tampering
  patterns:
  - pattern: cursor.execute($QUERY)
    pattern-not: cursor.execute($QUERY, $PARAMS)
  severity: ERROR
- id: security-custom-4
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: "Uncontrolled Resource Consumption \u2013 Brute Force Attack"
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-400
    owasp: A05:2021
    threat_category: Denial of Service
  patterns:
  - pattern: eval($X)
  - pattern: exec($X)
  severity: ERROR
- id: security-custom-5
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Exposed Sensitive Data in Logs
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-200
    owasp: A01:2021
    threat_category: Information Disclosure
  patterns:
  - pattern: print($SENSITIVE)
  - pattern: console.log($SENSITIVE)
  severity: ERROR
